arts.sum: AC Milan's star forward Rafael Leao faces a spell on the sidelines after sustaining a thigh injury during the recent match against Verona. arts.sum: Roma manager Claudio Ranieri has admitted he will be looking at Everton in a different light ahead of January, now that the Friedkin family have officially taken charge at Goodison Park. Il Messaggero cover comments from the Italian boss from his press conference on Friday in which he admits Everton are now in a different The post Claudio Ranieri hints at Everton and Roma business after Friedkin takeover – Looking at Toffees with interest now appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Friedkins arrive at Everton while facing flak at Roma after bright start
arts.sum: American father and son began positively at the Stadio Olimpico but botched managerial decisions soured mood A banner raised by Roma supporters before their Coppa Italia game at home to Sampdoria on Wednesday spoke to the prevailing mood. “Club and players: all a bunch of frauds,” it read. By recent standards, that was rather tame. The Friedkin Group, which completed its takeover of Everton on Thursday, has owned Roma since 2020 and enjoyed notable successes: hiring José Mourinho as manager and delivering him the squad he needed to win the first Europa Conference League, as well as finishing as Europa League runners-up a year later. However, the decision to fire the Portuguese in January divided opinion among the fanbase, and the sacking of his successor, Daniele De Rossi, in September sparked open revolt. Continue reading...
